title: Conjecture 1.1 (Tomescu, 1971)
statement_text: Let $G$ be a connected $k$-chromatic graph with $k \geq 4$. Then $G$ has at most $k!(k-1)^{|G|-k}$ $k$-colourings. Moreover, the extremal graphs are precisely the graphs obtained from $K_k$ by adding trees rooted at each vertex of the clique.
context_text: Tomescu conjectured in 1971 that the bound $x_k(x-1)^{n-k}$ for the number of $x$-colourings — attained by graphs formed from $K_k$ by growing arbitrary trees from each vertex — holds for every connected $k$-chromatic graph, whether or not it contains a $k$-clique, as long as $k \geq 4$. The requirement $k \neq 3$ is necessary since odd cycles of length at least 5 exceed this bound.
paper_contribution: Proves a strengthening of the conjecture for $k = 5$: every connected 5-chromatic graph $G$ satisfies $Q_G(y) \leq (y+1)y^{|G|-4}(y-1)(y-2)(y-3)$ for every integer $y \geq 4$, with equality characterised by graphs obtained from $K_5$ by adding a tree on each vertex.
paper_title: Maximum number of colourings. II. 5-chromatic graphs
